question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged user inject a TopicMessage into the Redis channel with a fee field set to a value that causes a precision loss when converted between numeric types during downstream processing, resulting in a fee value that is systematically lower or higher than the actual consensus fee?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged user supply a topicId with getId() returning a value that causes String.format('topic.%d', ...) to produce a channel name that is a prefix or suffix match for another topic's channel name, causing the Redis subscription to inadvertently receive messages from the wrong channel including fee-manipulated data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the lack of message ordering guarantees in Redis pub/sub to inject a fee-manipulated TopicMessage out of sequence, causing downstream consumers to process an incorrect fee for a transaction that has already been finalized with a different fee value?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the onBackpressureBuffer(maxBufferSize, BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR) in SharedTopicListener to flood the Redis channel with fee-manipulated messages, causing the buffer to overflow and legitimate fee messages to be dropped, resulting in an incomplete or incorrect fee history for downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged user publish a TopicMessage to the Redis channel with a fee field that is valid JSON/protobuf but contains an extra or unexpected field that the RedisSerializer<TopicMessage> maps to the fee attribute, causing an unintended fee value to be set in the deserialized object?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the doOnCancel() -> unsubscribe() -> topicMessages.remove() flow to repeatedly evict and re-create subscriptions for a target topic channel, creating a window during each re-subscription where attacker-staged fee-manipulated messages are the first to be delivered?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a consensus timestamp check in the Redis message pipeline to inject a TopicMessage with a future timestamp and a manipulated fee, causing downstream consumers to accept the message as a valid future transaction with an incorrect fee?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the computeIfAbsent() call's lack of authorization checks on the topicId to subscribe to any topic channel, including those belonging to other users, and observe fee data from those channels to inform targeted fee manipulation attacks?
